thread.title: Punjab Governor Launches 'Punjab Raj Bhavan Sankalp' Magazine to Promote Welfare Initiatives | Chandigarh News - Times of India
thread.title_full: Punjab Governor Launches 'Punjab Raj Bhavan Sankalp' Magazine to Promote Welfare Initiatives | Chandigarh News - Times of India

author: TNN
title: Punjab Governor Launches 'Punjab Raj Bhavan Sankalp' Magazine to Promote Welfare Initiatives | Chandigarh News - Times of India
text: Chandigarh: Punjab governor and UT administrator Gulab Chand Kataria an insightful quarterly magazine ‘Punjab Raj Bhavan Sankalp,’ that captures the essence of various activities of Punjab & UT Chandigarh. An initiative of Punjab Raj Bhavan & UT Chandigarh, this publication not only chronicles the governor’s visits across both regions, but also highlights numerous welfare schemes and awareness programmes designed to uplift communities and improve the quality of life for residents. Upcoming editions will carry information about welfare schemes. tnnWe also published the following articles recentlyPunjab, Hry slug it out over ChandigarhThe Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) in Punjab, led by Finance Minister Harpal Singh Cheema, has voiced strong opposition to Haryana's proposal to construct a legislative assembly complex in Chandigarh. Cheema emphasized that Chandigarh holds immense emotional significance as Punjab's capital and criticized Haryana's proposal as an attempt to infringe upon their rights.115346625New CMs oath may not take place at Raj Bhavan, say officialsFollowing the recent elections in Maharashtra, the location for the new council of ministers' swearing-in ceremony remains undecided. The chosen venue will depend on the new chief minister's preference, with speculation suggesting a location outside Raj Bhavan. The governor will play a crucial role in government formation, particularly if no alliance secures a clear majority.115575295First song from Raj Chakrabarty's upcoming film releasedRaj Chakrabarty's new film 'Shontan,' featuring a stellar cast including Mithun Chakraborty and Subhashree Ganguly, delves into the complexities of familial relationships. The recently released song 'Thakur Thakbe Kotokhon,' sung by Amit Kumar, sets the stage for the drama with visuals of Durga Puja festivities.115385936
